solution.solution_text: Let y = f(f(f(x))) + [f(x)]2. Step 1: y' = f'(f(f(x))) · f'(f(x)) · f'(x) +2f(x)f'(x) (chain + power rule). Step 2: At x = 1: f(1) = 1 ⇒ f(f(1)) = 1 ⇒ f(f(f(1))) = 1. Step 3: First term: 3·3·3 = 27. Second term: 2·1·3 = 6. y′(1) = 27 + 6 = 33.

At x = π/2: sin x = 1, cos x = 0, cot x = 0, ln 1 = 0. y1' = (π/2)^1 (0 · ln π/2 + 1) = π/2 · 2/π = 1. y2' = 1 · (0 + 0) = 0. y' = 1 + 0 = 1.

questions.question_text: The function f(x) = log(1+x) - (2x)/(2+x) is increasing on

solution.solution_text: Solution: Domain: 1 + x > 0 ⇒ x > -1, and x ≠ -2 (denominator). But -2 < -1, so domain x > -1. Step 1: f'(x) = 1/(1+x) - (2(2+x)-2x) / ((2+x)^2) = 1/(1+x) - 4 / ((2+x)^2). Step 2: Common denominator (1+x)(2+x)^2: f'(x) = ((2+x)^2 - 4(1+x)) / ((1+x)(2+x)^2) = (4+4x+x^2-4-4x) / ((1+x)(2+x)^2) = x^2 / ((1+x)(2+x)^2). For x > -1, x^2 ≥ 0, (2+x)^2 > 0, 1+x > 0. So f'(x) ≥ 0 for all x in domain. Thus, f is increasing on (-1, ∞).

solution.solution_text: Solution: Partial fractions: 1/((x+2)(x^2+1)) = A/(x+2) + (Bx+C)/(x^2+1). 1=A(x^2+1)+(Bx+C)(x+2).x=-2:1=A(4+1)=5A ⇒ A=1/5.Expand:Ax^2+A+Bx^2+2Bx+Cx+2C= (A+B)x^2+(2B+C)x+(A+2C). solution.solution_text: Solution: Let L = lockdown, C = controlled in one month. P(L)=0.7, P(no L)=0.3. P(C|L)=0.8, P(C|no L)=0.3. Total P(C) = P(C|L)P(L) + P(C|no L)P(no L) = 0.8·0.7 + 0.3·0.3 = 0.56 + 0.09 = 0.65.

questions.question_text: If f(x) = {x^2-1, 0 < x < 2; 2x+3, 2 ≤ x < 3} , the quadratic equation whose roots are lim_(x→2-) f(x) and lim_(x→2+) f(x) is

solution.solution_text: Solution: Left limit: x → 2-, f(x) = x^2 − 1 → 4 − 1 = 3. Right limit: x → 2+, f(x) = 2x + 3 → 4 + 3 = 7. Roots 3 and 7. Quadratic: (x - 3)(x - 7) = x^2 − 7x − 3x + 21 = x^2 - 10x + 21 = 0.

questions.question_text: Let the relation R is defined in N by a R b, if 3a + 2b = 27 then R is

solution.solution_text: Solution: N includes positive integers (1,2,3,...). Solve 3a + 2b = 27, a, b ∈ N. b = (27-3a)/2, must be positive integer. 27 - 3a even, positive; a odd (since 3a odd if a odd, 27 odd, odd-odd=even). a ≥ 1. If 3a is odd, then a must be odd. 3a ≤ 27-2 = 25. 3a is odd and ≤ 25. So 3a can be 3, 9, 15, 21. This gives a = 1, 3, 5, 7. Possible a: 1,3,5,7. a=1: b=(27-3)/2=24/2=12. a=3: b=(27-9)/2=18/2=9. a=5: b=(27-15)/2=12/2=6. a=7: b=(27-21)/2=6/2=3. Pairs: {(1,12),(3,9),(5,6),(7,3)}.

questions.question_text: If A = [[0,1],[0,0]] then (aI + bA)^n is (where I is the identity matrix of order 2)

options.B: a^n I + n a^(n-1) b A
options.C: a^n I + n · a^(n-1) b · A

correct_answer.answer_text: a^n I + n a^(n-1) b A

solution.solution_text: Solution: aI + bA = [[a, b],[0, a]]. This is upper triangular, powers: A = [[0,1],[0,0]]. A^2 = [[0,0],[0,0]]. A is nilpotent (A^2 = 0). (aI + bA)^n = Σ_(k=0)^n (nCk) (aI)^(n-k) (bA)^k. Since A^2 = 0, terms for k ≥ 2 are 0. So (aI + bA)^n = (nC0)(aI)^n (bA)^0 + (nC1)(aI)^(n-1) (bA)^1 = 1·a^n I + n·a^(n-1) b A. Since A = [[0,1],[0,0]], n a^(n-1) b A = [[0, n a^(n-1) b],[0, 0]]. So (aI +bA)^n = [[a^n, n a^(n-1) b],[0, a^n]] = a^n I + n a^(n-1) b A.

solution.solution_text: Solution: The determinant is given as: |[[1, -2, 5],[-2, a, -1],[0, 4, 2a]]| = 86. Expand along first row or third row. Along third row: 0(...) -4(1(2a) -5(-2)) + 2a(-2·1 -1·(-2)) = 86. -4(2a + 10) + 2a(-2+2) = 86. -8a - 40 + 2a(0) = 86. -8a - 40 = 86. -8a = 126. a = -126/8 = -63/4. This gives only one value for 'a'. Better along first column or compute. det = 1 · |[[a, -1],[4, 2a]]| - (-2) · |[[-2, -1],[0, 2a]]| + 0 · |[[-2, 5],[0, 4]]|. = 1(a·2a - (-1)·4) + 2(-2·2a - (-1)·0). = (2a^2 + 4) + 2(-4a). = 2a^2 + 4 - 8a. So 2a^2 - 8a + 4 = 86. 2a^2 - 8a - 82 = 0. a^2 - 4a - 41 = 0. This is a quadratic equation. Sum of roots = -(-4)/1 = 4.
